channel.title: Home on Peter Gao
channel.description: Recent content in Home on Peter Gao

item.title: Graduate Climate Conference 2020
item.description: Excited to share that I’ve been helping with planning for this fall’s (online) Graduate Climate Conference and applications are now open! Please share and feel free to send me any suggestions about making online conferences accessible/meaningful!

item.title: About Me
item.description: I am currently pursuing a PhD in Statistics at the University of Washington. My research interests center upon the uses of spatial and spatiotemporal statistics in environmental science and the social sciences. I am currently researching statistical postprocessing methods for improving forecasts for sea ice thickness with Adrian Raftery and Cecilia Bitz. I graduated from the University of Chicago in 2017 with an MS in Statistics and a BS in Mathematics and Statistics.

item.title: Other Links
item.description: This school year, John Best and I are organizing the Space/Time Reading Group on spatiotemporal statistics and related topics.

item.title: Space-Time Reading Group
item.description: This year, Space-Time Reading Group is jointly organized by John Bestand Peter Gao. To receive updates about meetings and other upcoming events, please sign up for the listhost. <p><tr ALIGN="Left"> <td> 11/27 </td> <td> Serge Aleshin-Guendel </td> <td> <a href="https://becarioprecario.bitbucket.io/spde-gitbook/ch-lcox.html" target="_blank">Ch. 4</a>: Point processes and preferential sampling </td>

item.title: Teaching
item.description: at the University of Washington: Teaching Assistant, STAT 394 Probability I (Winter 2020) Teaching Assistant, STAT 220 Principles of Statistical Reasoning (Spring 2018) Teaching Assistant, STAT 221 Statistical Concepts and Methods for the Social Sciences (Winter 2018) Teaching Assistant, STAT 221 Statistical Concepts and Methods for the Social Sciences (Fall 2017) In Summer 2019, I taught Applied Math and Logical Reasoning to middle school students as Junior Faculty at BEAM Discovery,
